item.title: Scott Pendlebury's 433rd game simply the latest in a long line of Collingwood star's AFL records
item.description: Scott Pendlebury is set to be the proud holder of the AFL's games record, but it's only the latest in a long line of records held by the Collingwood icon.

item.title: Photographer accused of stealing Bondi terror attack victim Peter Meagher's camera
item.description: A photographer who allegedly stole a camera from a Bondi terror attack victim before pawning it days later has been charged.

item.title: Strathmerton fears it will become 'ghost town' as Bega Cheese factory closure nears
item.description: Bega Cheese's Strathmerton factory, which employed more than 300 workers, is set to close its doors on June 30, leaving residents in the small Victorian town wondering what's next.

item.title: X asks court to dismiss Andrew and Tristan Tate's bid to unmask anonymous critics
item.description: Misogynist influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have struck a hurdle in their bid to unmask anonymous users they allege have defamed them, with social media platform X asking a Florida court to dismiss the motion.

item.title: Is Canberra's CBD unsafe? It depends who you ask
item.description: ACT industry groups say drug use and widespread construction are among issues eroding feelings of safety.

item.title: Australian rugby 7s co-captain Isabella Nasser inspired by 2016 Olympics victory
item.description: When Isabella Nasser tuned in to watch the 2016 Rio Olympics as a 13-year-old, she had little idea of the pathway her life was about to take.

item.title: Paul Keating backs CGT changes as 'structurally sound' — as it happened
item.description: Former prime minister Paul Keating says the government's changes to capital gains tax are "structurally sound" and seek to "arrest" the growth in house prices.

item.title: NT health authorities MIA as diphtheria spreads across Indigenous remote communities
item.description: As diphtheria spreads across four different Australian jurisdictions in the largest outbreak since records began, public health messaging in the NT — the region most impacted by the serious disease — has been hard to come by.

item.title: Iran examining US proposal as Donald Trump says talks in 'final stages'
item.description: Iran says it is examining the latest US proposal received from mediator Pakistan in talks to end the Middle East war, despite the two sides trading threats over resuming attacks.

item.title: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing will pay more tax under CGT changes
item.description: Gen Zs who take a lottery ticket approach to investing in the stock market will likely pay more tax under the capital gains tax changes being proposed by the government.

item.title: Congo football team cancels Kinshasa World Cup farewell as Ebola outbreak concerns grow
item.description: The Congo football team cancels its farewell in Kinshasa as the US clarified what the rules would be for players and staff entering the country for the World Cup.

item.title: Abrolhos Islands coral reef shows resistance to climate bleaching, study finds
item.description: In the midst of a mass coral bleaching event along the West Australian coast, a group of scientists have discovered a section of reef that has been able to withstand the heat.

item.title: Retiring NRL star Jai Arrow says he will fight after shock MND diagnosis
item.description: Jai Arrow reveals that MND has already affected his speech after announcing yesterday he was stepping away from the NRL after his shock diagnosis.

item.title: Outback landholders opening gate to large-scale renewable projects
item.description: If you mention renewable energy to agricultural producers in many regional communities, you will likely ruffle some feathers. But in far west NSW, landholders are showing preliminary support for a planned expansion of solar and wind energy assets.

item.title: What does broken heart syndrome feel like?
item.description: Experts say broken heart syndrome accounts for about two per cent of patients presenting with symptoms similar to a heart attack.

item.title: Prince William goes wild as Aston Villa claims Europa League title and ends 30-year drought
item.description: In front of a joyful future king of England, Aston Villa coach Unai Emery reaffirmed his status as the king of the Europa League as Villa beat Freiburg 3-0 in Istanbul to secure Emery a record-extending fifth title in the Europa League.

item.title: Birth choices dwindle as new laws push Geraldton's last private midwife to press pause in WA
item.description: Soon-to-be parents in regional WA will have less choice over where and how they give birth as care options diminish, midwives say.

item.title: On the eve of Stephen Colbert's final Late Show, America is asking: Can late night survive the Trump administration?
item.description: Like other checks and balances on presidential power, late-night talk shows — once ratings juggernauts, woven into the fabric of American culture — are being stress-tested. Here's how they became a staging ground for the battle over free speech.

item.title: Students return to Cairns high school as police revoke emergency declaration
item.description: Police have revoked an emergency declaration under the Public Safety Preservation Act after a Cairns high school was evacuated following reports of a threat.

item.title: Joey Chestnut to defend hot-dog-eating crown on probation after battery plea
item.description: The competitive-eating sensation was accused of slapping a man across the face during a late night out at an Indiana bar, according to Us Weekly.

item.title: Tech giant Nvidia reports growth of 85 per cent due to the construction of AI factories
item.description: The world's most valuable company, tech giant Nvidia, has reported a record revenue but investors are not impressed with second-quarter sales forecasts.

item.title: Unemployment rate jumps to 4.5 per cent, driven by female unemployment
item.description: All four major banks now expect the RBA to keep the cash rate on hold at its next meeting on June 16, as the unemployment rate spikes to 4.5 per cent.

item.title: Five reasons Stephen Colbert is one of the most important satirists in American history
item.description: The best satirists do more than entertain. They influence public discourse and leave lasting marks on political life.

item.title: Earthquakes hit near regional Queensland town twice in four days
item.description: Experts say this week's seismic activity is unusual, but the region has a pattern of large and clustered earthquakes.

item.title: Football Australia posts record losses as plan to axe 20 per cent of staff revealed
item.description: About 20 per cent of Football Australia staffers face the axe as soccer's governing body posts record losses that doubled last financial year's numbers.

item.title: Murder accused John Yalu told police a pensioner was 'pressuring' him to amputate his leg
item.description: A jury is deciding whether John Yalu, 41, committed murder when he used a circular saw to amputate the leg of a pensioner who had been "pressuring" him to remove the limb in exchange for $5,000.

item.title: Australia's largest diphtheria outbreak prompts $7.2m federal vaccine blitz
item.description: The federal government has stepped in to address the largest diphtheria outbreak since records began, unveiling a multi-million dollar package for more vaccines and workers to deliver them.

item.title: Australia backs landmark UN climate change ruling as others try to block it
item.description: Australia joins 140 other countries in passing a major United Nations resolution backing a landmark legal ruling on climate change, despite efforts by the United States, Russia and Saudi Arabia to sink it.

item.title: Kylie doco is a human portrait of pop royalty's resilience and reinvention
item.description: "Our Kylie" gets candid in the three-part Netflix docuseries, grieving over IVF struggles, Michael Hutchence, and revealing she was diagnosed with cancer a second time in 2021.

item.title: Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.15L of wine before fatal hit-run gets reduced jail term on appeal
item.description: An Adelaide lawyer who drank 1.1 litres of wine before killing a pedestrian in a fatal hit-run crash has won a reduced sentence on appeal — in part because she has a young child.

item.title: Bail refused after guns, antisemitic material allegedly seized from Central Coast home
item.description: A man has been refused bail after NSW Police allegedly seized antisemitic material, firearms and explosives from a home on the Central Coast.

item.title: Woman accused of hit-and-run murder of Kiesha Thompson at Daisy Hill committed to stand trial
item.description: Larissa Rita Mae-Leigh Sant is accused of planning to kill her then-partner's ex-girlfriend, Kiesha Thompson, before striking her with a car while she was walking.

item.title: Man convicted of Irma Palasics's death was previously jailed over million-dollar cigarette conspiracy
item.description: New details have emerged about the violent 1999 home invasion that claimed Irma Palasics's life, including a separate million-dollar cigarette conspiracy that put one of her killers behind bars a decade later.

item.title: Peak body slams ministers' absence from Aboriginal housing and homelands conference as 'disgraceful'
item.description: The Northern Territory's peak body for Aboriginal housing says it's "disgraceful" ministers from both levels of government have failed to attend a conference on the housing crisis.

item.title: Liberal leader Angus Taylor spruiks gas extraction in Adelaide as SA party opposes fracking proposal
item.description: After telling an Adelaide oil and gas conference "we need to get busy digging and drilling", Opposition Leader Angus Taylor bats away questions about the SA Liberal Party's resistance to fracking in the state's South East.

item.title: Israeli minister's taunting of activists no surprise to those following his career
item.description: Benjamin Netanyahu has condemned a video of his national security minister taunting activists, including Australians, who have been detained. But it's the prime minister who allowed this behaviour to percolate.

item.title: Family's story of dementia shines spotlight on Australia's leading cause of death
item.description: Gwen Knox, from Western Australia, has written a play inspired by her family that she hopes starts important conversations about dementia and truth-telling.

item.title: Jai Arrow's MND diagnosis shines spotlight on debilitating condition that has no cure
item.description: As another rugby league player is diagnosed with motor neurone disease, we asked experts why so little is known about what causes it.

item.title: Government doing 'everything in our power' to keep SA safe from paedophile Peter Liddy
item.description: The South Australian government has filed two applications to either ensure notorious paedophile and former magistrate Peter Liddy is indefinitely detained or the subject of an extended supervision order.

item.title: Arafura Rare Earths mine green-lit as federal government agrees to buy 500 tonnes of product per year
item.description: Arafura Rare Earths has announced its flagship project north of Alice Springs will go ahead, decades after it was first flagged as a prospect.

item.title: Tasmanian state budget 2026: Government to slash 1,700 jobs as Eric Abetz looks to rein in spending
item.description: The Tasmanian government will slash 1,700 jobs through natural attrition and negotiated voluntary redundancies, as Treasurer Eric Abetz unveils his second state budget.

item.title: Tasmanian state budget 2026: Where are the cuts coming from?
item.description: Treasurer Eric Abetz wants to find $1.47 billion in "efficiencies" in Tasmania's state budget. This is what's on the chopping block.

item.title: Perth and WA council mergers are on the table again — here's what happened last time
item.description: In 2015 the Barnett government abandoned plans to merge Perth councils — but now the idea has reared its head again. Here's what you need to know.

item.title: The Besties say violent podcast clip is 'just one example of what we deal with' as female creators
item.description: A video circulated across social media of a group of men laughing about how one of them would "beat the f***" out of a group of female content creators.

item.title: Coles investigates Apple gift card scam after Melbourne artist's viral complaint
item.description: Coles is looking into a Melbourne mother's complaint about faulty Apple gift cards worth $2,000.

item.title: AFLW's 2026 fixture to feature women's and men's double-headers
item.description: A series of double-headers involving the women's and men's competitions will take place after the AFLW season begins in August.

item.title: Public transport to stay free for an additional year in Tasmania
item.description: The Tasmanian government has committed $24 million in the state budget towards keeping buses and ferries free for one more year.

item.title: NDIS integrity inquiry hears raft of alarming claims and calls for greater whistleblower protections
item.description: A raft of alarming evidence has been aired at a parliamentary hearing into the integrity of the NDIS, with calls for the government to strengthen whistleblower protections.

item.title: Isabelle Kelly returns to chase an Origin dynasty 10 years after breakthrough Blues win
item.description: Ten years after playing in New South Wales's breakthrough victory over Queensland, Isabelle Kelly returns to the Gold Coast on the cusp of launching a Blues Origin dynasty.

item.title: Man sentenced for killing Ian Welldon in Toowoomba truck and motorcycle crash
item.description: The man had a "very high" level of methamphetamine in his blood when he crashed into the back of Ian Welldon's motorbike while failing to stop at a red light.

item.title: Albanese's budget backlash tour spends little time with Coalition
item.description: For a group of people who declared they were prepared to tackle a post-budget scare campaign, the government has looked anything but in control this week.

item.title: Prison term looms for ex-AFL footballer Nick Stevens as victims tell court of impact
item.description: A Victorian court hears former AFL player Nick Stevens was a blue-collar worker but committed a white-collar crime, ahead of his sentencing for defrauding six families in 2017.

item.title: SpaceX files for largest IPO in history, with potential to make Elon Musk the world's first trillionaire
item.description: Elon Musk's sprawling space exploration company, which also owns Starlink and xAI, was valued at $US1.25 trillion ($1.75 trillion) earlier this year, meaning Musk could become the world's first trillionaire if the offering goes ahead at that valuation.

item.title: Man charged with five counts of rape after alleged break-in at Adelaide Hills home
item.description: A man accused of breaking into an Adelaide Hills home and sexually assaulting a person unknown to him has been charged with multiple counts of rape, court documents show.

item.title: 'Emotional' Hawks kick off Tassie farewell as star Crow injured
item.description: Blake Hardwick emerges the unlikeliest of heroes for Hawthorn as the Hawks record a nine-point win over Adelaide in Tasmania.

item.title: Pauline Hanson proposes new gas tax and public stake in drilling projects
item.description: The One Nation leader wants a flat royalty on the production of new gas projects.

item.title: Tasmanian state budget 2026: Ratings agency S&P warns budget savings 'hard to achieve' as others react
item.description: Credit rating agency S&P Global says Tasmania's budget repair ambitions face "high execution risk". The government aims to save $1.5 billion through cuts.

item.title: 'I've never been so confused': Raiders slam officials, fall to Dolphins in thriller
item.description: The Raiders huffed and puffed but couldn't overcome the depleted Dolphins, who despite missing six key players through injury and Origin won a fourth-straight NRL game for the first time.

item.title: Thursday finance with Daniel Ziffer
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: School in mouse-infested WA town closed after toxic bait spread on grounds
item.description: Students of the Western Australian School of Agriculture in the WA town of Morawa are sent home and the school is closed after toxic mouse bait intended for paddocks is spread on the campus.

item.title: Price denies agreeing migrants from Asia, Africa and Middle East are 'flooding' Australia
item.description: Jacinta Nampijinpa Price has denied that she agreed with a podcaster saying Australia should stop 'flooding' Australia with migrants from Asia, Africa and the Middle East.

item.title: The Mushroom Tapes, Mad Mabel triumph at Australian Book Industry Awards
item.description: In May 2025, Helen Garner, Chloe Hooper and Sarah Krasnostein, three literary true-crime writers, headed to Erin Patterson's trial. The resulting book just won an Australian Book Industry Award.

item.title: Another group of Australians with IS links leave camp in Syria for Australia
item.description: The ABC has seen buses carrying the group, which left the al-Roj camp on Thursday afternoon, local time.

item.title: Air France and Airbus found guilty of involuntary manslaughter for Rio-Paris flight crash
item.description: France's national airline Air France and Airbus have both been found guilty of involuntary manslaughter over a 2009 crash that killed 228 people.

item.title: Harry Connick Jr and Jessica Gethin share a Carnegie Hall moment
item.description: Harry Connick Jr's new symphony, Elaboratio, is filled with musical tributes to his mother Anita. Orchestra Victoria and Australian conductor Jessica Gethin are bringing those memories to life at Carnegie Hall.

item.title: ABC news quiz: A pumped-up prince, a grinning sculpture, a damaged icon and a stowaway frog
item.description: You'll be as happy as Prince William when you take this week's quiz, which goes off like a frog in a … bag of lettuce.

item.title: Safety top priority as drone shows return to Vivid Sydney light festival
item.description: Dazzling drone shows will return to Sydney's largest art and culture festival for the first time since 2024.

item.title: ASIO cut counterterrorism funding share to post-9/11 low before Bondi attack
item.description: Australia's intelligence agencies had cut counterterrorism funding to its lowest share this century before the Bondi massacre, despite warnings that a terrorist attack was probable and that antisemitic violence posed an escalating threat to life.

item.title: Welcome to Robinvale, the town where people are not counted
item.description: In a community like no other, what happens when people aren't counted? Welcome to Robinvale, where these workers and their families don't exist.

item.title: Iran tightens grip on Strait of Hormuz with new transit regime
item.description: Iran is asserting unprecedented control over the strategically critical waterway, with ships reportedly facing screening processes, transit approvals and potential Bitcoin-backed insurance requirements.

item.title: Treasurer asked to explain productivity impact of tax changes
item.description: The Productivity Commission was not asked to model how changes to capital gains tax would impact Australia's economy. Business groups say Treasurer Jim Chalmers must explain how the government concluded its tax changes would ultimately be a boon to productivity.

item.title: One Nation surfs wave as Labor handles budget disquiet
item.description: Before the budget, Anthony Albanese and Jim Chalmers knew they would be hit by criticism for its broken promises, but they underestimated the extent and ferocity of the backlash.

item.title: Snowy Hydro bosses paid more than $1.2m in bonuses as renewables project costs spiral
item.description: The renewables project's chief executive, Dennis Barnes, received $323,000 in bonus pay on top of his $1.69 million salary last financial year, while four other senior executives received about $919,000 in "variable pay".

item.title: CVs losing value for hiring as use of artificial intelligence changes industry, recruiters say
item.description: AI-perfected CVs are becoming the norm as more job applicants and employers use artificial intelligence, but recruiters say the technology could be making hiring trickier.

item.title: Caravan repairers battling to stay afloat amid fuel crisis impacting customers
item.description: Normally at this time of year, RVs are hitting the road to head north and escape winter. But the ongoing fuel crisis is forcing travellers to stay closer to home, causing a serious economic downturn for many of the 2,500 businesses in the RV repair industry.

item.title: Cubans describe life under oil blockade and uncertainty over America's next move
item.description: As power and petrol supplies run out, Cubans tell the ABC about their daily lives. "You can no longer find streets packed with travellers, music and dance," one says. "Only Cuban nationals, still attempting to take some bread home."

item.title: Sweeping cuts but little detail — can Tasmanian government stick to Treasurer Eric Abetz's budget?
item.description: Since Eric Abetz became Tasmania's treasurer nine months ago, there have been two prevailing questions. How much will be cut? And what will be cut? As of yesterday, we know how much, but don't know what or where.

item.title: Algal bloom, fishing restrictions see seafood prices rise as King George Whiting hits $100 per kilo
item.description: South Australia's peak seafood body says a short supply of some species due to the algal bloom and commercial fishing restrictions has led to slight price increases, as it warns it could take industry five to 10 years to fully recover.

item.title: Optus fails to warn Coleambally residents about 13-day planned phone outage
item.description: Optus has apologised after failing to notify a western Riverina town about a 13-day planned outage for service upgrades.

item.title: Cuba accepts offer of $140m in American aid, Rubio says
item.description: Tensions between the longtime Cold War rivals have hit a new low in recent days after the US indicted the country's 94-year-old former president Raúl Castro.

item.title: What do we know about how changes to family trusts affect farmers?
item.description: Despite winning a carve-out to changes to family trusts announced in the budget, farmers are still worried about what tax reform means for them.

item.title: Parents of children in NT foster care say concerns around black eyes, bite marks ignored by department
item.description: The parents of two children in Northern Territory foster care have spoken out about their "heartbreaking" experience with the system, saying concerns their daughter was being abused by her foster family were brushed off by the department.

item.title: Four-metre waves on the way as south-east Queensland braces for beach erosion
item.description: Large swells driven by a Tasman Sea low are expected to bring hazardous surf and further erosion to already affected Gold Coast beaches over the weekend, before easing early next week.

item.title: PM leaves door open to discretionary trust tax changes — as it happened
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has left the door open to tweaking a proposed change to how a discretionary trust is taxed.

item.title: Guzman Y Gomez soars on US exit, ASX climbs higher — as it happened
item.description: Australian markets have risen this morning, with Guzman Y Gomez surging on news it has pulled out of the US market.

item.title: Sale's premier sporting export Scott Pendlebury almost never played AFL
item.description: Scott Pendlebury is about to set the record for most AFL matches played. This is the latest milestone in a stellar career that almost never was.

item.title: City of Perth Lord Mayor Bruce Reynolds tight-lipped amid council turmoil
item.description: It's been labelled "dysfunctional" and "diabolical". What does the lord mayor have to say about the embattled City of Perth?

item.title: Arsenal coach Mikel Arteta says he cooked barbecue instead of watching game that gave him EPL title
item.description: Mikel Arteta says he decided to not watch the match that secured Arsenal an historic EPL title, instead deciding to cook a barbecue.

item.title: US-bound plane diverted after passenger from Ebola-hit Congo boards 'in error'
item.description: The US has introduced new restrictions on travellers from countries exposed to an Ebola outbreak in Africa, requiring them to enter through Washington DC.

item.title: Footage shows 'fireball' meteor lighting up skies in parts of eastern Australia
item.description: Colourful streaks across the sky have been spotted across eastern Australia as a "fireball" meteor passed through the atmosphere.

item.title: Meteor lights up skies over NSW
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: New Adelaide University study finds many purebred dingoes contain some domestic dog genetics
item.description: A new study into dingo DNA has found many animals previously considered to be purebred actually have domestic dog genetics.

item.title: NASCAR champion Kyle Busch dies aged 41 after 'severe illness'
item.description: Kyle Busch, who won two Cup Series championships and will be a future NASCAR Hall of Famer, has died aged 41.

item.title: What is 'pink-slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?
item.description: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink-slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

item.title: Almost 1.2 billion people living with mental disorders worldwide as case numbers nearly double since 1990
item.description: A surge over the last three decades means mental disorders now account for more than 17 per cent of all disability worldwide, according to a report published this week.

item.title: Murray-Darling inflow laws secure water for delicate ecosystems, ecologist says
item.description: Controversial new legislation could secure critical water supplies for delicate ecosystems while delivering benefits to the Murray-Darling Basin's agricultural sector, a leading river ecologist says.

item.title: Farmers struggle to source cottonseed for cattle feed amid dry conditions
item.description: A by-product of the cotton harvest, cottonseed boosts protein and fibre in animal diets. But as graziers ramp up their drought preparations, dry conditions are causing a supply slowdown.

item.title: Dermatologist drought in central Queensland leaves patients with few treatment options
item.description: Regional Queensland is facing a shortage of dermatologists, leaving some patients increasingly desperate.

item.title: Full-day strike flagged for June as Canberra public school teachers wrap up two-hour stoppage
item.description: Canberra public schools were closed until 10:30am today, with more industrial action expected in June.

item.title: Queensland cricket club gives women a safe and inclusive place to play
item.description: A Queensland cricket team, started by a mother who wanted to give her autistic daughter a safe space to play, has just embarked on an international tour.

item.title: Hydra, a female platypus, was among four released into the Royal National Park this week.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Platypus population in Australia's oldest national park increases to at least 20
item.description: There are at least 20 platypuses now living in the NSW Royal National Park's waterways, three years after their historic reintroduction.

item.title: Is it worth getting a robotic lawnmower?
item.description: Experts say robot lawnmowers are becoming more accessible, but there are some key features to look out for.

item.title: Farm worker John Yalu found not guilty of murdering Kalman Tal in circular saw amputation
item.description: The jury has returned its verdict in the case of a man who killed pensioner Kalman Tal. The 66-year-old died shortly after paying $5,000 to have his leg amputated in a park.

item.title: Brumby fertility control trial considered as aerial shooting to resume in Kosciuszko National Park
item.description: Wild horse numbers in the NSW Snowy Mountains are back up with a reproductive control trial now being considered as aerial shooting has been confirmed to resume.

item.title: Australian dies in Peru after fall on Inca Trail to Machu Picchu
item.description: Australian man Matthew Paton, a Victoria Police sergeant, has died in Peru after falling up to 400 metres from a mountain path while walking the Inca Trail to Machu Picchu.

item.title: India's Cockroach Janta Party amasses support of millions in just a few days
item.description: A satirical political party in India amasses the support of millions in just a few days and politicians are taking notice.

item.title: Why Kotoni Staggs will take things personally on his return to State of Origin
item.description: When Kotoni Staggs takes the field he transforms into the angriest man in the NRL and upon his return to the New South Wales side the Brisbane centre is ready to channel his rage into State of Origin.

item.title: College graduation speaker booed over AI comments
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Roger Cook warns Kimberley fracking may be needed if Woodside's Browse gas project fails
item.description: Roger Cook raises the prospect of gas fracking in WA's Kimberley region, warning the state could face future energy shortfalls if Woodside Energy's massive Browse project does not proceed.

item.title: Bathurst men charged after police find alleged bomb-making lab in house near Bathurst
item.description: Three men have been charged with explosives and firearms offences as specialist police teams search a home in a quiet regional street.

item.title: Diphtheria outbreak could be 'contained within months' but response criticised as too slow
item.description: Rapid vaccination could stem the spread of the potentially deadly diphtheria outbreak, but the government's response has been criticised as being too slow.

item.title: Coach Trevor Gleeson joins Bryce Cotton at Adelaide 36ers
item.description: One of Australian basketball's most successful coaches and one of the national league's greatest players have paired in Adelaide.

item.title: Secrecy surrounds legal bid to block release of IBAC corruption report
item.description: The release of a corruption report into Victorian government has been blocked, but the public has been denied the right to know by whom.

item.title: Kalyn Ponga explains why he's in the 'sweet spot' for the Maroons ahead of Origin opener
item.description: Kayln Ponga was a controversial selection for the Maroons, but the Newcastle star says he's backing himself to perform.

item.title: Alice Springs School of the Air celebrates 75 years of remote education
item.description: The world's oldest school of the air is celebrating its 75th anniversary in Alice Springs. It provides a service to families over 1.3 million square kilometres and also is known as the world's largest classroom.

item.title: Controversial 76-metre-tall 'Arc de Trump' design one step closer to approval. Here's what we know
item.description: The Arc de Triomphe-inspired arch in Washington DC is meant to commemorate the US's 250th anniversary but its location is causing controversy.

item.title: Australian killed while climbing Machu Picchu
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Last moments of murdered Tasmanian police officer Keith Smith heard in Leigh Sushames sentencing hearing
item.description: A court hears that some of the last words uttered by a policeman was to ask the man who fatally shot him, "how are you, mate?"

item.title: China and Russia have again blocked moves to protect the endangered emperor penguin
item.description: The annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations has failed to reach consensus to designate the endangered emperor penguin as a "Specially Protected Species".

item.title: Lachlan Freund and Tina Hazard jailed for 2022 manslaughter of Thor Morgan in Toowoomba
item.description: Two people have been jailed for their role in the shooting death of 25-year-old Thor Morgan, who was attacked in a suburban Toowoomba street in 2022.

item.title: Woman who died in house fire 'rejected' accused murderer, Victorian Supreme Court hears
item.description: The prosecution alleges the 62-year-old farmstay guest was "unable to cope" after the deceased declined to enter into a romantic relationship with him.

item.title: Staff exodus continues at Albury Wodonga Health as controversial development moves on
item.description: The exodus of executives is continuing at embattled Albury Wodonga Health, with the board chair announcing he will not be seeking reappointment when his term ends.

item.title: Tolu Koula to make Origin debut at stadium where his parents competed in Sydney Olympics
item.description: Manly speedster Tolu Koula is set to bring his pace to the Origin arena at the same stadium where his parents represented Tonga in the Sydney Olympics.

item.title: Residents hopeful as first stage of Jervis Bay flyover opens
item.description: After almost a decade of campaigning, residents welcome safety improvements at a notorious stretch of the Princes Highway considered the most crash-prone turn-off in southern New South Wales.

item.title: Woman jailed over boat crash on River Murray at Mannum
item.description: A woman who was likely more than three times the legal blood alcohol limit when she ran over a swimmer while driving a boat has been jailed for more than two years.

item.title: Friend of Rottnest Island shark attack victim Steven Mattaboni says there was 'no warning' before death
item.description: The best friend of a Perth father killed by a shark off Rottnest Island says there was "absolutely no warning" before the attack.

item.title: Australian Islamic State-linked families arrive in Damascus from al-Roj camp
item.description: After leaving the al-Roj refugee camp on Thursday, a group of seven Australian women and 14 children has arrived in the Syrian capital, Damascus.

item.title: Melbourne man found guilty of keeping woman as a slave
item.description: The court heard the man had beaten the woman and deprived her of food while forcing her into unpaid labour at his home.

item.title: Sentencing of Daniel Billings for murder of Molly Ticehurst delayed
item.description: The defence team for Daniel Billings has submitted evidence from a forensic psychiatrist that found he was in an "abnormal state of mind" when he killed his former partner.

item.title: Pacific Islands leaders endorse fuel taskforce plan amid concerns about oil supply
item.description: A taskforce will see leaders get a better idea of threats to energy deliveries, while Australia and New Zealand provide expert analysis on future supply.

item.title: Bulldogs coach slams 'bulls***' rumours after downing Storm
item.description: Matt Burton has inspired inspired the Canterbury Bankstown Bulldogs to a 30-20 come-from-behind victory against the Melbourne Storm, snapping their five-game losing streak.

item.title: 'Noticeable crack': Dons skipper cops horrific injury in Tigers loss
item.description: Andrew McGrath is set to be sidelined with a broken jaw after Essendon's loss to Richmond, as Fremantle marched on with a victory over the Saints.

item.title: Friday finance with David Chau
item.description: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

item.title: Labor leaves door open to an additional trust tax exemption
item.description: The federal government has left the door open to further changes after coming under pressure for including future discretionary testamentary trusts in a new tax announced in the budget.

item.title: US pauses Taiwan weapons sale over Iran war, acting navy boss says
item.description: The stalled $19.6 billion weapons purchase by Taiwan has been awaiting sign-off from US President Donald Trump.

item.title: Tony Abbott returns to political life as Downer drops out of race for Liberal Party president
item.description: Former prime minister Tony Abbott will be nominated unopposed to become the next federal president of the Liberal Party.

item.title: Tax slug threatens clean energy 'flight' despite record federal support, says investor lobby
item.description: As the federal government races towards its 2030 renewable energy goal, investors warn capital gains taxes changes risk upending the entire effort.

item.title: Putin vows retaliation as Luhansk student dorm strike death toll rises to 10
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin has accused Ukraine of a "terrorist strike" on a student dormitory in the Russian-controlled Luhansk region, with a local official saying 10 were killed and 48 others injured.

item.title: Mosab Abu Toha documents life in Gaza under Israeli occupation in his poetry collection, Forest of Noise
item.description: The month he turned 30, Mosab Abu Toha was blindfolded, beaten and detained by the Israel Defense Forces. The essay he wrote about it won a Pulitzer Prize.

item.title: Agave industry gains momentum as growers explore potential of spiky succulent beyond spirits
item.description: Australia's emerging agave industry is attracting interest from distillers, farmers and scientists who believe the spiky plant could transform everything from tequila production to sustainable aviation fuel.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Test your knowledge of Prince, Twin Peaks and some very important donkeys
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: The race to save a conjoined twin
item.description: A team of doctors involved in the separation of conjoined Papua New Guinean twins Sawong and Tom have spoken for the first time about the rare and complex case.

item.title: What's with all the videos of college graduates booing AI?
item.description: It's graduation season for universities across the US, which has bred an unexpected viral trend: graduates jeering at artificial intelligence.

item.title: Former CEO of Google Eric Schmidt speaks about AI in a university commencement speech
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Crowd boos at community college graduation after AI system fails
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Mexico's ancient ball sport ulama's revival in spotlight ahead of FIFA World Cup
item.description: Ulama remains an important part of Mexican identity, with authorities leveraging the momentum of the FIFA World Cup to shine a spotlight on the ancient sport once again.

item.title: Israel misses the point and consequences of its actions in wake of flotilla outcry
item.description: In the wake of an outcry over its treatment of detained activists, Israel has focused on messaging as its actions undermine its support from the international community.

item.title: The budget's central weakness was revealed this week
item.description: As debate over the federal government's budget tax hit on wealth and assets continues to reverberate, one can't help but wonder how it's going down among the gen Zs and millennials it's designed to impress.

item.title: The Interview: Playwright Suzie Miller stood at a crossroad and chose the risky path
item.description: In 2009, Suzie Miller had a choice between becoming a magistrate in Australia or a residency at the National Theatre in London.

item.title: ASIO assessment did not warn about heightened Hanukkah risks before Bondi attack
item.description: Australia's intelligence agency, ASIO, issued its annual holiday threat assessment less than two weeks before the Hanukkah terrorist attack at Bondi Beach but did not identify heightened risks to the Jewish festival, despite earlier declaring antisemitism as its top priority in terms of threat to life.

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard resigns as US President Donald Trump's director of national ​intelligence
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard says she is departing from the role to support her husband after his recent bone cancer diagnosis.

item.title: SA energy minister spruiks fracking potential, but south-east farmers unconvinced
item.description: A decade's worth of gas supply for South Australia is potentially sitting below the Limestone Coast region. But will it ever be extracted?

item.title: Willie Rioli Junior goes home to the Tiwis just when the islands' football club needs saving
item.description: The Tiwi Islands are unique and Australian Rules football is a way of life that has spawned some of the game's biggest names. But even as favourite son Willie Rioli Jr returns home, the Tiwi Bombers know they need more help.

item.title: Rural women share harrowing IVF stories at parliamentary inquiry
item.description: Farmer and mother of two Sophie Fletcher has endured miscarriages, hours of travel and false hope throughout her IVF journey. She wants to see the system change for rural women.

item.title: Proposed Territory Plan changes to allow 'missing middle' housing on all ACT residential blocks
item.description: Townhouses, terraces and low-rise apartments will be allowed on all ACT residential blocks under a major change to planning rules proposed by the government.

item.title: Shares, start-ups, small businesses and ETFs: What Labor's capital gains tax changes mean in practice
item.description: Labor's capital gains tax changes have set off a firestorm of debate about the implications for small businesses, start-ups, share owners and plenty more. Here's who will really pay more.

item.title: Residents concerned about future of The Palms development in Far North Queensland
item.description: A developer blames the Iran war for his decision to axe a water park and resort project in regional Queensland despite already spending $21 million.

item.title: WHO upgrades risk assessment for Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo to 'very high'
item.description: The World Health Organization raises its assessment of the outbreak going nation-wide as a province in the Congo's east bans funerals and suspends the local football league.

item.title: As Jarrod Bleijie goes on the attack, does David Crisafulli have oversight of his deputy?
item.description: Queensland's deputy premier made a 'dick' joke about his political opponent this week, but the difference in his approach and the premier's raises questions, writes political reporter Jack McKay.

item.title: As Scott Pendlebury broke AFL's games record he also helped restore Collingwood's superpower status
item.description: Scott Pendlebury's record-breaking career has spanned almost five separate Collingwood eras, with each one helping put the club back near the top of the AFL's pecking order.

item.title: Victoria's major party leaders outline election agenda with duelling pledges six months out from poll
item.description: Premier Jacinta Allan and Opposition Leader Jess Wilson deliver their pre-election promises as both parties hold their state conferences.

item.title: Kangaroo Island's little penguin numbers rise after record low in 2024
item.description: Little penguin numbers on Kangaroo Island have rebounded, but estimates show there are only about 700 adult breeders after a harmful algal bloom.

item.title: Australia's two-party system 'already collapsed' with the rise of One Nation, analyst says
item.description: With the rise of One Nation's popularity and younger voters increasingly turning to the Greens, the traditional two-party system in Australian politics has been dismantled, a former political strategist argues.

item.title: Indigenous artist Tony Albert's optimism is an act of generosity
item.description: Girramay, Yidinji and Kuku-Yalanji artist Tony Albert hopes to "take every item of 'Aboriginalia' in Australia out of circulation".

item.title: A Collingwood champion's achievement has reopened an old wound outside Victoria
item.description: When Scott Pendlebury steps onto the MCG on Saturday afternoon, his name will stand alone at the top of a remarkable list — but the achievement has also reignited a debate about the value of non-Victorian Aussie Rules records.

item.title: UN expert answers Bright primary students' burning climate change questions
item.description: Primary school students in regional Victoria have their climate change questions answered.

item.title: Sabra Lane interviews Tasmanian Treasurer Eric Abetz for Stateline
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Health Minister denies health cuts, despite asking department to find $700m in cuts
item.description: Health groups fear the impact of hundreds of millions of dollars in cuts expected to be found in the Health Department as part of Treasurer Eric Abetz's state budget operational efficiencies program.

item.title: Some of Australia's most isolated residents live with black mould and broken toilets in government housing
item.description: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

item.title: Antarctic station discovers its shipping containers floating away on iceberg after blizzard
item.description: Details of how the shipping containers ended up on a giant iceberg have been revealed in one of the more unusual reports tabled at the annual meeting of Antarctic Treaty nations.

item.title: NT tourism and hospitality businesses call for COVID-era vouchers as businesses close down
item.description: A popular Darwin restaurant has closed down and the owner of another local hospitality business is asking for COVID-era support measures to be brought back as costs rise and visitor numbers fall.

item.title: Police investigate triple fatal road crash in Sans Souci, southern Sydney
item.description: Three people have died in a car crash in Sydney's south, the ABC understands one of the vehicles was likely a rideshare car.

item.title: Albany's abandoned WWII fuel tanks get new life as global graffiti gallery
item.description: Tucked away in bushland on Western Australia's south coast, a hidden World War II site has become a sprawling canvas for street artists from all over the world.

item.title: Alex Albon hits groundhog during Canadian Grand Prix F1 practice session
item.description: A groundhog is killed and an F1 car heavily damaged after a collision during practice for the Canadian Grand Prix.

item.title: Bushfires could 'exploit' doormat for entry to your home, fire expert warns
item.description: Preparing for an ember attack could extend your home's bushfire survivability rate to above 90 per cent or even closer to 100 per cent, a fire scientist says.

item.title: 'Life-changing' surf therapy program launched in Queensland to help female veterans
item.description: A Queensland woman has launched a program designed specifically for women veterans to improve their mental health.

item.title: Workforce shortages contribute to psychiatrist's lighter penalty after he sexually harassed nurse
item.description: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

item.title: Qld man charged by Joint Counter Terrorism Team for alleged 'concerning posts'
item.description: A 48-year-old has been arrested by the state's Joint Counter Terrorism Team over alleged "concerning posts" on social media.

item.title: Vaccination 'not enough' to contain diphtheria spread in remote WA, disease official says
item.description: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

item.title: Call to 911 reveals the moments leading up to NASCAR champion Kyle Busch's tragic death
item.description: A call to 911 reveals NASCAR champion Kyle Busch had been coughing up blood in the hours before his tragic death aged 41.

item.title: Tech sector warns tax could "double" under CGT changes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX launches upgraded Starship
item.description: Elon Musk's rocket company has launched its new Starship design to enable more frequent Starlink satellite launches and to send future NASA missions to the moon.

item.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman's tech titan rivalry moves from the courtroom to Wall Street
item.description: After Sam Altman and OpenAI's recent victory in a drawn-out legal battle with billionaire Elon Musk, the bitter rivalry between the former friends is now set to move from the courtroom to Wall Street.

item.title: The perfect warm winter salad using crisp-roasted artichokes and grains - ABC News
item.description: Crispy artichokes and textural grain make this salad a satisfying dinner and excellent next-day lunch.

item.title: Children's entertainer Danny Go announces death of 14-year-old son from cancer in emotional Instagram tribute
item.description: The popular children's YouTuber revealed the news after documenting the illness since it was diagnosed late last year. Tributes have flowed from Children's entertainers, including the Wiggles.

item.title: Tech sector onslaught against proposed changes to capital gains tax could see start-ups carved out
item.description: Start-up founders say the federal government's CGT changes may see investment in new businesses go offshore. But Airtree Ventures co-founder Daniel Petre is warning against a scare campaign.

item.title: Shipbuilding woes and tired submarines — why there's more talk of an AUKUS 'plan B'
item.description: Defence industry experts argue Australia should consider how to prevent a naval "capability gap" — or risk being left exposed.

item.title: Live: 'There's more in the tank:' Pendlebury celebrates games record despite Eagles scare
item.description: Collingwood's No.10 enjoys a 10-point win in his record-breaking match as the Magpies hold firm to down the Eagles at the MCG. Follow live.

item.title: Brent Harvey reflects on losing AFL/VFL games record to Collingwood star Scott Pendlebury
item.description: Brent 'Boomer' Harvey has enjoyed being the AFL/VFL games record holder for the past decade, and says he could have played more if he'd accepted an offer from other clubs to extend his career.

item.title: Take off and landing of SpaceX's Starship V3
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Two-vehicle crash leaves three dead in Sydney’s south
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Take of SpaceX's Starship V3
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland jockey Shane McGovern dies in hospital after training accident
item.description: Both of Shane McGovern's legs were amputated during a weeks-long stay in hospital.

item.title: Darwin man Martin Wiberg dies in hospital months after alleged bashing at Casuarina bus stop
item.description: Friends of Darwin man Martin Wiberg, 48, are remembering him as a "very gentle soul" after he died in hospital this week.

item.title: Charges withdrawn against Michael Agzarian, who had Nazi symbols in artwork
item.description: The case has been dropped against an Australian artist charged with displaying Nazi symbols on an artwork depicting politicians and billionaires.

item.title: Death toll jumps to 90 after Chinese coal mine blast
item.description: A gas explosion at a coal mine in northern China's Shanxi province has jumped to 90, state media reports. Nine people remain missing.

item.title: Tax changes add to softening house sales caused by interest rate rises, agents say
item.description: After riding a post-COVID wave of booming prices, real estate agents say SA's housing market is starting to level out, as federal budget tax changes and rising interest rates reduce buyer appetite.

item.title: Live: More boos at home for winless Dragons after another loss to Warriors
item.description: St George Illawarra is 0-11 to start the 2026 season after losing to the Warriors in Kogarah, where the locals let their feelings be known. Follow live.

item.title: One Nation would become federal opposition if election held today, poll predicts
item.description: One Nation would win dozens of seats if a federal election was held today, according to a major opinion poll.
